meta.title: Troubleshooting Guide
meta.excerpt: Common issues, debugging techniques, and resolution strategies for production systems.

seo.metaDescription: Debug and resolve common production issues quickly.

blocks.content: Systematic troubleshooting requires understanding your system's logs, metrics, and error patterns.

blocks.title: High CPU Usage
blocks.content: Check for runaway loops, memory pressure, and expensive database queries.

blocks.title: Production Debugging
blocks.content: Test remediations in staging before rolling changes to production.
Follow a systematic debugging workflow to identify and resolve issues efficiently.

items: Reproduce the issue in a controlled environment
Check recent deployments and code changes
Review application logs for errors and warnings
Examine system metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network)
Verify external service availability and latency

features.title: Reproduce
features.description: Isolate the issue in a controlled environment

features.title: Investigate
features.description: Gather logs, metrics, and error traces

blocks.content: Effective log analysis helps quickly identify root causes and patterns in production issues.

blocks.title: Common Log Analysis Commands
blocks.code:
# View recent errors in Vercel logs
vercel logs --follow

# Filter for specific error patterns
vercel logs | grep "ERROR"

# Search for specific request ID
vercel logs | grep "request-id: abc123"

# Count error occurrences
vercel logs | grep "ERROR" | wc -l

# View application logs with timestamps
pnpm run dev | bunyan -o short

blocks.title: Structured Logging
blocks.content: Use structured logging with consistent fields (timestamp, level, requestId, userId) to enable powerful log queries and aggregations.

blocks.title: Build Failures
blocks.content: Check for TypeScript errors, missing dependencies, or environment variable issues. Review build logs in CI/CD pipeline.

blocks.title: API Timeout Issues
blocks.content: Increase timeout limits, optimize slow queries, implement caching, or add request retry logic with exponential backoff.

blocks.title: Memory Leaks
blocks.content: Use Node.js heap snapshots, check for unclosed connections, review event listener cleanup, and monitor memory metrics over time.
